action.exact_quote: "Confidential Information" means information disclosed by or on behalf of a Discloser that is marked confidential at the time of disclosure or confirmed as confidential in writing within ten days after disclosure. "Purpose" means evaluating, negotiating, and, if agreed, performing the contemplated data integration between the parties. Confidential Information includes written, electronic, oral, visual, technical, financial, product, customer, operational, and business information.
action.proposed_redline: Confidential Information means all non-public information disclosed by or on behalf of a party, whether before or after the effective date, in any form or medium and whether or not marked confidential.
action.rationale: Lexical hint matched by grep baseline.

observation.client_context: Client is evaluating a strategic data integration with a potential vendor. The parties will exchange personal data under this NDA, and the client wants a balanced mutual NDA reviewed against the NDA playbook.
observation.instruction: Review the mutual NDA against the playbook. Flag deviations, quote the clause, propose fallback text when required, and escalate missing information.

playbook.fallback: Confidential Information means all non-public information disclosed by or on behalf of a party, whether before or after the effective date, in any form or medium and whether or not marked confidential.
playbook.position: Confidential Information should cover all non-public information disclosed by either party, whether or not marked, and should include oral, visual, written, electronic, and other disclosed information.

playbook.fallback: Each party and its controlled affiliates may disclose or receive Confidential Information under this Agreement, and each party is responsible for its affiliates' compliance.
playbook.position: The NDA should apply mutually to both parties and their affiliates when affiliates disclose or receive information.

playbook.fallback: The exclusions apply only to information the recipient can document was lawfully known without restriction, publicly available through no breach, independently developed without use of Confidential Information, or received from a third party without breach of duty.
playbook.position: Exclusions should be limited to information already known without restriction, public through no breach, independently developed, or received from a lawful third party without duty.

playbook.fallback: The recipient may use Confidential Information solely to evaluate, negotiate, and, if agreed, perform the contemplated transaction between the parties.
playbook.position: Use should be limited to evaluating and negotiating the contemplated transaction.

playbook.fallback: Recipient will protect Confidential Information using at least reasonable care and no less than the care it uses to protect its own similar confidential information.
playbook.position: The care standard must be at least reasonable care and no less than the recipient uses for its own similar confidential information.

playbook.fallback: Recipient may disclose Confidential Information to Representatives only on a need-to-know basis, provided those Representatives are bound by written or professional duties at least as protective as this Agreement.
playbook.position: Disclosures to representatives should be need-to-know only, and representatives must be bound by confidentiality duties at least as protective as the NDA.

playbook.fallback: Before any compelled disclosure, Recipient will, to the extent legally permitted, provide prompt notice and reasonable cooperation so Discloser may seek protective treatment.
playbook.position: Compelled disclosure should require prompt notice where legally permitted and reasonable cooperation to seek protective treatment.

playbook.fallback: Upon request, Recipient will promptly return or destroy Confidential Information, except archival or legal-hold copies retained under continuing confidentiality obligations.
playbook.position: Return or destruction should occur promptly upon request, with archival and legal-hold copies remaining protected.

playbook.fallback: Confidentiality obligations continue for three years after disclosure, and trade secrets remain protected for so long as they qualify as trade secrets under applicable law.
playbook.position: Confidentiality obligations should last at least three years, and trade secrets should remain protected as long as they remain trade secrets.

playbook.fallback: No license, assignment, or ownership interest is granted by disclosure of Confidential Information except the limited right to use it as expressly permitted by this Agreement.
playbook.position: The NDA should not grant licenses or ownership rights in Confidential Information.

playbook.fallback: Each party acknowledges that unauthorized use or disclosure may cause irreparable harm for which equitable relief may be appropriate, without limiting other remedies.
playbook.position: The agreement should preserve equitable relief for unauthorized use or disclosure.

playbook.fallback: Delete the residual knowledge clause unless expressly approved by the client in writing.
playbook.position: Residual knowledge clauses should be absent unless expressly approved by the client.

playbook.fallback: No change; absence of a non-solicit covenant is acceptable.
playbook.position: A standard NDA does not require a non-solicit covenant; the absence of one is acceptable and should not be flagged.

playbook.fallback: No change; New York governing law is acceptable for this smoke-test NDA.
playbook.position: New York governing law is acceptable for this engagement and should not be flagged as an issue.

action.exact_quote: Each party's controlled affiliates may disclose or receive Confidential Information under this Agreement, and each party is responsible for its controlled affiliates' compliance with this Agreement.
action.proposed_redline: Each party and its controlled affiliates may disclose or receive Confidential Information under this Agreement, and each party is responsible for its affiliates' compliance.
action.rationale: Lexical hint matched by grep baseline.

playbook.position: Exclusions should be limited to information already known without restriction, public through no breach, independently developed, or received from a lawful third party without duty.

playbook.fallback: The recipient may use Confidential Information solely to evaluate, negotiate, and, if agreed, perform the contemplated transaction between the parties.
playbook.position: Use should be limited to evaluating and negotiating the contemplated transaction.

playbook.fallback: Recipient will protect Confidential Information using at least reasonable care and no less than the care it uses to protect its own similar confidential information.
playbook.position: The care standard must be at least reasonable care and no less than the recipient uses for its own similar confidential information.

playbook.fallback: Recipient may disclose Confidential Information to Representatives only on a need-to-know basis, provided those Representatives are bound by written or professional duties at least as protective as this Agreement.
playbook.position: Disclosures to representatives should be need-to-know only, and representatives must be bound by confidentiality duties at least as protective as the NDA.

playbook.fallback: Before any compelled disclosure, Recipient will, to the extent legally permitted, provide prompt notice and reasonable cooperation so Discloser may seek protective treatment.
playbook.position: Compelled disclosure should require prompt notice where legally permitted and reasonable cooperation to seek protective treatment.

playbook.fallback: Upon request, Recipient will promptly return or destroy Confidential Information, except archival or legal-hold copies retained under continuing confidentiality obligations.
playbook.position: Return or destruction should occur promptly upon request, with archival and legal-hold copies remaining protected.

playbook.fallback: Confidentiality obligations continue for three years after disclosure, and trade secrets remain protected for so long as they qualify as trade secrets under applicable law.
playbook.position: Confidentiality obligations should last at least three years, and trade secrets should remain protected as long as they remain trade secrets.

playbook.fallback: No license, assignment, or ownership interest is granted by disclosure of Confidential Information except the limited right to use it as expressly permitted by this Agreement.
playbook.position: The NDA should not grant licenses or ownership rights in Confidential Information.

playbook.fallback: Each party acknowledges that unauthorized use or disclosure may cause irreparable harm for which equitable relief may be appropriate, without limiting other remedies.
playbook.position: The agreement should preserve equitable relief for unauthorized use or disclosure.

playbook.fallback: Delete the residual knowledge clause unless expressly approved by the client in writing.
playbook.position: Residual knowledge clauses should be absent unless expressly approved by the client.

playbook.fallback: No change; absence of a non-solicit covenant is acceptable.
playbook.position: A standard NDA does not require a non-solicit covenant; the absence of one is acceptable and should not be flagged.

playbook.fallback: No change; New York governing law is acceptable for this smoke-test NDA.
playbook.position: New York governing law is acceptable for this engagement and should not be flagged as an issue.

issues.exact_quote: "Confidential Information" means information disclosed by or on behalf of a Discloser that is marked confidential at the time of disclosure or confirmed as confidential in writing within ten days after disclosure. "Purpose" means evaluating, negotiating, and, if agreed, performing the contemplated data integration between the parties. Confidential Information includes written, electronic, oral, visual, technical, financial, product, customer, operational, and business information.
issues.proposed_redline: Confidential Information means all non-public information disclosed by or on behalf of a party, whether before or after the effective date, in any form or medium and whether or not marked confidential.
issues.rationale: Lexical hint matched by grep baseline.

issues.exact_quote: Each party's controlled affiliates may disclose or receive Confidential Information under this Agreement, and each party is responsible for its controlled affiliates' compliance with this Agreement.
issues.proposed_redline: Each party and its controlled affiliates may disclose or receive Confidential Information under this Agreement, and each party is responsible for its affiliates' compliance.
issues.rationale: Lexical hint matched by grep baseline.

issues.exact_quote: Confidential Information does not include information that Recipient can document was known to Recipient without restriction before disclosure, becomes public through no breach of this Agreement, is independently developed without use of Confidential Information, or is received from a third party without breach of a duty to Discloser.
issues.proposed_redline: The exclusions apply only to information the recipient can document was lawfully known without restriction, publicly available through no breach, independently developed without use of Confidential Information, or received from a third party without breach of duty.
issues.rationale: Lexical hint matched by grep baseline.

issues.exact_quote: Recipient may disclose Confidential Information to its directors, officers, employees, attorneys, accountants, consultants, financing sources, and other advisers (collectively, "Representatives") who have a need to know the information for the Purpose.
issues.proposed_redline: Recipient may disclose Confidential Information to Representatives only on a need-to-know basis, provided those Representatives are bound by written or professional duties at least as protective as this Agreement.
issues.rationale: Lexical hint matched by grep baseline.

issues.exact_quote: Upon Discloser's written request, Recipient will return or destroy Confidential Information within ninety days, except one archival copy may be retained by legal counsel for recordkeeping and legal-hold purposes, subject to this Agreement.
issues.proposed_redline: Upon request, Recipient will promptly return or destroy Confidential Information, except archival or legal-hold copies retained under continuing confidentiality obligations.
issues.rationale: Lexical hint matched by grep baseline.

issues.exact_quote: This Agreement begins on the effective date and continues for two years. Recipient's confidentiality obligations continue for three years after each disclosure, and trade secrets remain protected for so long as they qualify as trade secrets under applicable law.
issues.proposed_redline: Confidentiality obligations continue for three years after disclosure, and trade secrets remain protected for so long as they qualify as trade secrets under applicable law.
issues.rationale: Lexical hint matched by grep baseline.

issues.exact_quote: All Confidential Information remains the property of Discloser. No license, assignment, or ownership interest is granted by disclosure except the limited right to use Confidential Information as expressly permitted by this Agreement.
issues.proposed_redline: No license, assignment, or ownership interest is granted by disclosure of Confidential Information except the limited right to use it as expressly permitted by this Agreement.
issues.rationale: Lexical hint matched by grep baseline.

issues.exact_quote: Recipient acknowledges that unauthorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information may cause irreparable harm for which monetary damages may be inadequate, and Discloser may seek equitable relief without limiting any other available remedy.
issues.proposed_redline: Each party acknowledges that unauthorized use or disclosure may cause irreparable harm for which equitable relief may be appropriate, without limiting other remedies.
issues.rationale: Lexical hint matched by grep baseline.

observation.client_context: Client is evaluating a strategic data integration with a potential vendor. The parties will exchange personal data under this NDA, and the client wants a balanced mutual NDA reviewed against the NDA playbook.
observation.instruction: Review the mutual NDA against the playbook. Flag deviations, quote the clause, propose fallback text when required, and escalate missing information.

playbook.fallback: Confidential Information means all non-public information disclosed by or on behalf of a party, whether before or after the effective date, in any form or medium and whether or not marked confidential.
